description: Continuous UX & feature improvements across admin dashboard, pro space, and public salon pages

userStories.title: Admin: Plan filter chips above salons list
userStories.description: As a super-admin, I want to filter salons by plan so I can quickly see which clients are on each tier.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 4 filter chips above #salonsList in admin/index.html: Tous / Starter / Pro / Premium
Clicking a chip calls a filterByPlan(plan) function in admin/js/admin.js
filterByPlan filters state.salons and calls renderSalonCards with filtered array
Active chip has a highlighted style (use --primary border/background)
Selecting 'Tous' clears the plan filter and shows all salons
Plan filter combines with existing text search (filterSalons still works independently)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Admin: Show salon count badge in salons page header
userStories.description: As a super-admin, I want to see how many salons are shown vs total so I know if filters are active.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a <span id='salonCount'> element in the salons page header next to the '🏪 Mes Clients' title
renderSalonCards() updates salonCount text to 'N salon(s)' where N is the filtered count
If filtered count differs from state.salons.length, show 'N / Total' format
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Pro: Earnings estimate stat card on dashboard
userStories.description: As a salon owner, I want to see my estimated earnings for the current month directly on the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a new stat card in #dashStats in pro/js/pro.js after the existing stat cards
The card shows the sum of prices for all bookings in the current month (from bookings API data already fetched in loadDashboard)
Label: 'Revenus du mois (est.)', value formatted as 'X CHF'
Use the same stat-card markup as the other cards (class='stat-card purple' or similar)
If no bookings data is available, show '— CHF'
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Pro: Upcoming bookings mini-list on dashboard
userStories.description: As a salon owner, I want to see my next 3 upcoming bookings on the dashboard at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a section 'Prochains rendez-vous' below #todayBookings in pro/index.html with id='upcomingBookings'
In loadDashboard() in pro/js/pro.js, fetch bookings for the next 7 days and show the first 3 upcoming ones
Each item shows: time, client name, service name, employee name (if available)
If no upcoming bookings, show a friendly empty state message
Section is hidden if all upcoming bookings are within today (avoid duplication)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Website: Quick-book button on service cards
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to click 'Réserver' directly on a service card to open the booking modal pre-selecting that service.
acceptanceCriteria: In applySalonBranding (website/js/app.js), add a small 'Réserver →' button inside each service card markup (makeCard function)
Clicking the button calls openBooking() and then pre-selects the service by setting bmState.service and calling goToStep for the datetime step
Button style: small, outline variant, sits at bottom of the card below service-meta
Does not appear on services with no price data
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Website: Scroll-to-top floating button
userStories.description: As a visitor on a long salon page, I want a scroll-to-top button that appears after scrolling down so I can quickly go back to the top.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a <button id='scrollTopBtn'> in website/index.html fixed at bottom-left (not conflicting with the FAB at bottom-right)
Button is hidden by default; appears when scrollY > 400px via scroll event listener in website/js/app.js
Clicking scrolls smoothly to top (window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' }))
Button styled as a small circle with '↑' icon, semi-transparent dark background, positioned bottom-left
Add CSS in website/css/style.css for #scrollTopBtn with transition for opacity/visibility
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Admin: Confirm dialog before deleting a salon
userStories.description: As a super-admin, I want a proper modal confirmation (not browser alert) before deleting a salon to prevent accidents.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the window.confirm() call in deleteSalon() in admin/js/admin.js with a modal confirmation
Show a modal (reuse existing showModal/closeModal pattern) with the salon name, a warning message, and Cancel / Supprimer buttons
The delete only proceeds when the user clicks Supprimer in the modal
Cancel closes the modal without deleting
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Pro: Toast notification on successful settings save
userStories.description: As a salon owner, I want clear feedback when my settings are saved so I know the action was successful.
acceptanceCriteria: After every successful PUT to /api/pro/salon/:salonId in pro/js/pro.js, call showToast() with a success message
Check all save handlers: saveBranding, saveHours, saveAddress, saveServices — each should call showToast on success
Toast message should be specific, e.g. 'Horaires enregistrés ✅', 'Adresse mise à jour ✅'
On API error, show showToast with an error message in red
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
